name: Salmon Salade Nicoise
recipeIngredient: 6 ounces green beans, cut into 3-inch pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Place salmon in a heavy medium skillet. Season with salt and pepper. Sprinkle with tarragon. Add enough water to the skillet to just cover the salmon. Cover and simmer the salmon over medium-low heat until the salmon is opaque but still pink in the center, about 7 minutes. Using a spatula, transfer the salmon to a plate and refrigerate until chilled. Reserve the water in the skillet.
2. Return the reserved water in the skillet to boil. Add the potatoes to the skillet. Cover and simmer until the potatoes are just tender when pierced with a knife, about 10 minutes. Transfer the potatoes to a plate. Cool and slice. Reserve the water in the skillet.
3. Add enough additional water to the skillet to cover the bottom by 1 inch. Bring the water to boil. Add the beans. Cover and simmer over high heat until the beans are crisp-tender, about 4 minutes. Rinse the beans under cold water to cool. Drain. (The salad can be prepared up to 6 hours ahead; refrigerate.)
4. Arrange lettuce leaves on each of two plates. Top each with a salmon fillet. Arrange half of the potatoes, green beans, tomatoes, and hard-boiled eggs decoratively around each salmon fillet. Sprinkle half of the green onions and capers over each salmon fillet. Serve, passing vinaigrette separately.
